mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier discounting and lower real-rate pressure on equity valuations, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because stable inflation lets lower policy rates transmit directly into multiples.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods with falling FEDFUNDS and stable CPIAUCSL, or if the conditional Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE marks labor-market deterioration that reduces earnings expectations and raises recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is triggered only when unemployment rises meaningfully from its recent baseline, not on small monthly noise.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the median forward return after a trigger is not below the unconditional median by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates stronger household demand and payroll income, which supports near-term corporate revenues and offsets discount-rate drag, leading to positive 5-day S&P 500 returns. The effect requires both demand and labor momentum together, not either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-signal quintile does not outperform the bottom quintile by at least 1.0 percentage point over 5-day horizons, or if either proxy alone explains the same effect without the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained pickup in INDPRO reflects improving industrial supply conditions and stronger cyclical output, which tends to lift earnings expectations and cyclicals within the S&P 500 over a one-quarter horizon. The signal works only when output growth is positive rather than merely less negative.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO change triggers fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average forward return is not above the unconditional mean by 0.75 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy pressure eases and equity duration extends, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when inflation re-accelerates or policy is already near the lower bound.
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation-triggered episodes do not deliver positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the signal is unprofitable after controlling for FEDFUNDS level.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST after a prior peak signals weakening housing demand and tighter credit transmission, which leads S&P 500 returns lower over the following quarter. The effect is strongest once housing starts have rolled over for at least two months.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 2 consecutive months after a 6-month high
failure_condition: Reject if rollover episodes fail to generate negative 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not below the unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS rises while UNRATE also rises, labor supply is not translating into tightness and the economy is absorbing workers poorly, which signals weakening demand and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. This contradiction in the labor data should matter more than either series separately.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term does not outperform single-series rules, or if periods with rising PAYEMS and rising UNRATE do not have below-baseline 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, simultaneous gains in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicate broad demand normalization, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as revenue expectations improve. The signal breaks when GDP advances without sales growth or sales recover without GDP growth.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-sales expansion regime fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of episodes, or if the interaction adds no predictive value over either series alone.
